prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Fix TestSiluMulGroupFp8QuantModel after W8A8 block linear refactor
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
The W8A8 block linear refactor (#33892) replaced `W8A8BlockFp8LinearOp` with `TestFP8Layer` in `TestSiluMulGroupFp8QuantModel` but didn't adapt the forward call: `TestFP8Layer.forward()` takes `(y, bias=None)` while the model was still passing `(y, self.w, self.wscale)`. The redundant weight/scale tensors were also leftover from `W8A8BlockFp8LinearOp` which didn't store its own weights, unlike `TestFP8Layer` which creates them internally.

Additionally, on non-fnuz ROCm (MI355) the fusion pattern expects the triton group quant op, but the kernel's `use_triton` flag was `False` for small test shapes. Fixed by wrapping `quant_fp8` to force `use_triton=True` independently of the gemm path.

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [ROCm] Cap Triton paged attention block size to fix ROCm shared memory OOM
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
Hybrid Mamba models (e.g. Jamba) inflate block_size to 2048 to align attention and Mamba page sizes. When the ROCm custom paged attention kernel rejects this (it only supports 16/32), the Triton fallback kernel_paged_attention_2d used 2048 as its tile size, requesting 262144 bytes of shared memory and thus exceeding the MI325X hardware limit of 65536 bytes. Cap TRITON_BLOCK_SIZE at 128. The kernel already decouples tile size from physical block size via l_block_idx/internal_offsets addressing, so this is safe.

### Test plan

- [x] `pytest tests/models/language/generation/test_hybrid.py`

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [ROCm] Widen OAI Triton MoE capability range to include gfx12 (RDNA4)
prs.author: laudney
prs.body_head:
## Summary

- Widen `_supports_current_device()` cap range from `< (11, 0)` to `< (13, 0)` in `BaseOAITritonExperts` and `OAITritonMxfp4ExpertsMonolithic`
- Same change in `oracle/mxfp4.py` for the LoRA path

gfx12 (RDNA4) maps to capability `(12, 0)` which was excluded by the old upper bound. Triton 3.5+ supports `tl.dot_scaled` on gfx12 via `DecomposeScaledBlocked`, so the standard MXFP4 MoE path works without any custom kernel.

Replaces #34632 which tried to add a custom dequant kernel — turns out that's unnecessary (thanks @ptrojahn for pushing back on that).

Tested with `openai/gpt-oss-20b` (MXFP4 MoE) on AMD Radeon AI PRO R9700 (gfx1201).

AI assistance was used. Test commands run: `curl localhost:8080/v1/chat/completions` against a running vLLM instance with the model loaded.

## Test plan

- [x] Load MXFP4 MoE model (gpt-oss-20b) on gfx1201 — model loads, correct output
- [ ] CI

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Fix `trust_remote_code` AttributeError in EAGLE3 acceptance length test
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
Fixes `test_eagle3_acceptance_length` in the `tests/v1/spec_decode` test group (V1 speculative decoding tests). The test's `get_mt_bench_prompts` helper built a `SimpleNamespace` to pass into `vllm.benchmarks.datasets.get_samples`, but omitted `trust_remote_code`. `get_samples` reads `args.trust_remote_code` when constructing the HuggingFace dataset, causing every parametrization of `test_eagle3_acceptance_length` to fail with:

```
AttributeError: 'types.SimpleNamespace' object has no attribute 'trust_remote_code'
```

This patch adds `trust_remote_code=False` to the namespace so the dataset can be constructed as intended.

### Testing

- [x] `pytest tests/v1/spec_decode/test_acceptance_length.py`

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [ROCm] Add UE8M0 scale packing for Triton silu_mul_quant
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
Adds UE8M0 (int32 packed) scale output support to the Triton `_silu_mul_fp8_quant_deep_gemm` fallback kernel on ROCm. Depends on:
- #37833

The Triton kernel computes float32 scales, then packs them into int32 UE8M0 format by extracting the float32 exponent bits (bits 30:23).

This is effectively dead code on ROCm today (UE8M0 requires DeepGEMM which is CUDA-only), but ensures feature parity and removes a test skip.

## Test plan

- [x] `test_silu_mul_fp8_quant_deep_gemm`: 23/23 passed (UE8M0 tests now run on ROCm instead of being skipped)

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [ROCm] Profiler api support for ROCm MORI toy proxy server in PD Disaggregation
prs.author: itej89
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
This PR adds profiler api support to ROCm MORI toy-proxy-server in PD Disaggregation setup

## Test Plan
Run VLLM PD Disaggregation using MORI KV Connector as below

**STEP1:** Run Proxy Server
```
python /app/vllm/examples/online_serving/disaggregated_serving/moriio_toy_proxy_server.py 
```

**STEP2:** Run Prefill Server
```
vllm serve ${MODEL_PATH} \
        -tp 1 \
         --data-parallel-size ${DECODE_DP_SIZE} \
        [. . .]
        --kv-transfer-config '{"kv_connector":"MoRIIOConnector","kv_role":"kv_producer","kv_port":"9711","kv_connector_extra_config":{"proxy_ip":"'"${MASTER_ADDR}"'","proxy_port":"10001","proxy_ping_port":"36367","http_port":"20005","local_ping_port":"61555","handshake_port":"8405","notify_port":"61005"}}' 

```
**STEP3:** Run Decode Server
```
 vllm serve ${MODEL_PATH} \
        -tp 1 \
        --data-parallel-size ${DECODE_DP_SIZE} \
        [. . .]
        --profiler-config.profiler torch \
        --profiler-config.torch_profiler_dir \"$decode_dir\" \
        --profiler-config.torch_profiler_with_stack true \
        --profiler-config.torch_profiler_with_flops true \
        --profiler-config.torch_profiler_with_memory true \
        --profiler-config.torch_profiler_record_shapes true \
        --profiler-config.torch_profiler_dump_cuda_time_total true \
        --kv-transfer-config '{"kv_connector":"MoRIIOConnector","kv_role":"kv_consumer","kv_port":"9711","kv_connector_extra_config":{"proxy_ip":"'"${MASTER_ADDR}"'","proxy_port":"10001","proxy_ping_port":"36367","http_port":"20005","local_ping_port":"61555","handshake_port":"8405","notify_port":"61005"}}' 
```
**STEP4:** Run Benchmark
```
 vllm bench serve \
           --model $MODEL_PATH \
           --backend vllm \
           --host 10.158.213.211 \
           --port 10001 \
           [. . .]
            --profile
```

## Test Result
Below Decode server log shows profiler being started and stopped with respective trace fi

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Fix CK MXFP4 MoE GEMM crash for unaligned intermediate_size_per_partition
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
Running test_mxfp4_loading_and_execution_moe[model_case0] (Qwen1.5-MoE-A2.7B with tp=2) on GFX950 crashes with "device_gemm with the specified compilation parameters does not support this GEMM problem". This happens because the CK MXFP4 MoE kernel requires both hidden_dim and intermediate_size_per_partition to be multiples of 256, but Qwen1.5-MoE has moe_intermediate_size=1408, which with tp=2 gives intermediate_size_per_partition=704 (704 % 256 = 192, not aligned).

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [CI] Revamp translation validation tests: parametrize ROCm backends, add seed, relax semantic assertions
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
- Replace `rocm_aiter_fa_attention` conftest fixture with inline pytest parametrization of ROCm attention backends (TRITON_ATTN, ROCM_ATTN, ROCM_AITER_FA on mi3xx). Non-ROCm runs get a single `None` (default) param.
- Split the old `server` fixture (which yielded a `(server, model_name)` tuple) into separate `model_name`, `attn_backend`, and `server` fixtures. Rename `client_and_model` to `client`.
- Add `SEED = 42` and `TEMPERATURE = 0.0` constants; pass them consistently to every request via `extra_body` (previously most requests had no seed).

### Assertion changes

| Test | Old assertion | New assertion | Effect |
|---|---|---|---|
| `test_basic_audio` | `"greek sea" in out` | `"greek sea" in out` + `"exile" in out` | **Stronger** |
| `test_basic_audio_with_lora` | `"pequeño" in out.split(" ")` | `"pequeño" in out` + `"mary" in out` | **Stronger** |
| `test_audio_prompt` | prompt text NOT in output (2 checks) | Same 2 NOT-in checks (whisper-only) + `"greek sea" in out` (all models) | **Stronger** |
| `test_long_audio_request` | `out.count("greek sea") == 2` | `out.count("greek sea") >= 2` | **Equivalent** |
| `test_streaming_response` | Fuzzy: ≥87% of words match | `streamed_text == expected_text` (strict equality) | **Stricter** |
| `test_stream_options` | `assert final and continuous` | Three separate asserts with messages | **Same logic** |
| `test_audio_with_max_tokens` | Same checks | Same checks + error messages | **Same logic** |

With deterministic seeding, all outputs are **bitwise identical across runs**. 

## Test plan

- [x] `pytest -s -v tests/entrypoints/openai/speech_to_text/test_translation_validation.py`

<details>
<summary>Detailed changelog</summary>

**Imports (lines 17-18)**: Removed `from tests.entrypoints.openai.conftest import add_attention_backend`. Added `from vllm.platforms import current_platform`.

**Module-level constants (lines 20-26)**: Added `SEED = 42` and `TEMPERATURE = 0.0` constants. `SERVER_ARGS` b

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Optimize ROCm Docker build: registry cache, DeepEP, and ci-bake script
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
## Summary

Implements the three-tier Docker build for ROCm CI. Every PR currently rebuilds RIXL, DeepEP, rocshmem, torchcodec, and RDMA libraries from scratch, costing a total of 26 minutes on average per build. This PR introduces a pre-built Tier-1 `ci_base` image that absorbs those stable layers. Per-PR builds then only rebuild the thin vLLM wheel + workspace layer.

**Image registry layout after this PR:**

| Tag | Built by | Frequency |
|-----|----------|-----------|
| `rocm/vllm-dev:base` | `Dockerfile.rocm_base` | Monthly |
| `rocm/vllm-dev:ci_base` | `ci_base` stage (this PR) | Weekly |
| `rocm/vllm-ci:$COMMIT` | `test` stage (this PR) | Per PR |

---

This PR is connected to: https://github.com/vllm-project/ci-infra/pull/307
These two PRs should likely be merged simultaneously.

cc @kenroche @okakarpa @tjtanaa @gshtras @khluu

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Patching docker mirrors amidst ubuntu archive outage
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Add K8s-hardened minimal Python CI runner with JUnit exit-code fix and resource watchdog
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
Replaces the 500-line bash `run-amd-test.sh` with Python script (`run-amd-test.py`) that is easier to read, debug, and extend. The bash script becomes a thin shim that delegates to the Python implementation.

The new script handles:
- `amd-smi` GPU integrity verification
- Docker pull with retry (3 attempts, exponential backoff)
- Container execution with configurable timeout; kills and returns exit code 124 on timeout
- Health watchdog with background thread samples memory, host memory, disk usage, GPU VRAM, and container state every 60s. It also writes pre/post snapshots and all samples to `health_watchdog.log`
- JUnit exit-code override that promotes exit 0 to 1 when `results.xml` reports failures (catches `os._exit(0)` masking real failures)

cc @kenroche @okakarpa @dhonnappa-amd @tjtanaa

prs.title: [ROCm] Fix AssertionError in ActivationQuantFusionPass when torch.compile is used on ROCm
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
PR #38817 changed the `SiluMulBlockQuantPattern` registration guard from `is_cuda()` to `is_cuda_alike()`, inadvertently enabling it on ROCm. However, this pattern requires `per_token_group_fp8_quant` in `QUANT_OPS` (from `rms_quant_fusion.py`), and that op is CUDA-only (`#ifndef USE_ROCM` in `csrc/libtorch_stable/torch_bindings.cpp`).

On ROCm, `QUANT_OPS` does not contain `kFp8Dynamic128Sym` or `kFp8Dynamic64Sym`, so `ActivationQuantFusionPass.__init__` crashes when torch.compile is used:
```
AssertionError: unsupported quantization scheme kFp8Dynamic128Sym
```

The pattern can never match on ROCm anyway since the unfused `per_token_group_fp8_quant` op does not exist there. ROCm uses `rocm_aiter_fusion.py` for its own block-quant fusions.

Fix: revert the `SiluMulBlockQuantPattern` loop guard to `is_cuda()`. The `FUSED_OPS` entry for `silu_and_mul_per_block_quant` remains `is_cuda_alike()` for potential future use.

**Duplicate check:** No existing open PR addresses this. This is a regression introduced in #38817 (merged Apr 8, 2026).

**Tests:** `ActivationQuantFusionPass` is only instantiated during `torch.compile`; verified the guard logic by tracing `QUANT_OPS` population in `rms_quant_fusion.py` (lines 82-84, CUDA-only) against the `is_cuda_alike()` check.

**AI assistance:** Authored with Claude Code assistance.

cc @jeffdaily @sunway513 @jithunnair-amd @pruthvistony @ROCmSupport @gshtras @hongxiayang @naromero77amd

prs.title: [ROCm] Fallback SwigluOAI activation when _C op is absent
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
## Summary
- add the same missing-`_C` fallback pattern to `SwigluOAIAndMul`
- switch `forward_cuda()` to use `self.op` so the class can cleanly fall back to `forward_native`
- add a focused regression test for ROCm/cuda-alike builds where `_C.swigluoai_and_mul` is absent

## Why this is not duplicating an existing PR
- I checked the live PR/issue surface for `swigluoai_and_mul` fallback work and did not find an open or merged PR covering this activation.
- This is a narrow follow-up to #39729, which applies the missing-`_C` fallback pattern to neighboring activations but does not touch `SwigluOAIAndMul`.

## Tests run
- `uv run --no-project --python 3.12 --with torch python -` with a stub loader around the real `custom_op.py` and `activation.py`
  - before the fix: reproduced `AttributeError: 'types.SimpleNamespace' object has no attribute 'swigluoai_and_mul'`
  - after the fix: `PASS`
- `uv run --no-project --python 3.12 python -`
  - `py_compile` succeeded for:
    - `vllm/model_executor/layers/activation.py`
    - `tests/kernels/core/test_activation.py`

## AI assistance
This PR was prepared with AI assistance.

prs.title: [ROCm] Add SWIGLUSTEP activation support to AITER fused MoE
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
Step-3.5-Flash uses SWIGLUSTEP (SwiGLU with hard clamp at 7.0) for certain MoE layers. The AITER MoE backend doesn't handle this activation type, so running Step-3.5-Flash on ROCm with AITER enabled hits:

```
ValueError: Unsupported activation: MoEActivation.SWIGLUSTEP
```

This maps SWIGLUSTEP to the existing AITER swiglu activation path in both the dispatch and `_supports_activation`. Same approach used by cutlass_moe, fused_moe, and deep_gemm_moe backends which all support SWIGLUSTEP already.

Workaround before this fix: `export VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_MOE=0`

Fixes #36193

Signed-off-by: Bortlesboat <bortlesboat@users.noreply.github.com>

prs.title: [ROCm] Add missing @enable_fake_mode to TritonAddRMSNormPadFusionPass
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
`RocmAiterTritonAddRMSNormPadFusionPass.__init__` is missing the `@enable_fake_mode` decorator that both sibling classes in the same file have:

- `RocmAiterRMSNormQuantFusionPass` (line 288) — has it
- `RocmAiterSiluMulFp8GroupQuantFusionPass` (line 389) — has it
- `RocmAiterTritonAddRMSNormPadFusionPass` (line 491) — **missing**

Without it, `get_inputs()` during pattern registration allocates real CUDA tensors via `self.empty(...)` and `torch.empty(...)` instead of fake tensors. This wastes GPU memory during compilation init and can fail if no GPU is available during tracing.

Also fixes a comment that incorrectly described the fused op's return order as \`(result, scale, residual)\` — the actual order from `_rocm_aiter_rmsnorm_with_add_fp8_group_quant_impl` is \`(result, residual, scale)\`.

No duplicate PRs found. AI assistance was used for code search; all changes reviewed by human submitter.

Signed-off-by: Bortlesboat <bortstheboat@gmail.com>

prs.title: [ROCm] Fix cu_seqlens_q off-by-one in AITER FA speculative decode path
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
In the speculative decode path of `AiterFlashAttentionImpl` (when `decode_max_query_len > 1`), `cu_seqlens_q` is sliced as `query_start_loc[:num_decodes]`, giving `num_decodes` elements. But `cu_seqlens_q` for `unified_attention` is a cumulative length array that needs `num_seqs + 1` entries (including the leading 0).

The correct pattern is already used in the fallback path 33 lines later (line 1228-1229), which correctly does `[:num_decodes + 1]`.

Also fixed `descale_shape` which computed `shape[0] - 1` on the wrong-length slice, producing `num_decodes - 1` instead of `num_decodes`. Simplified to use `num_decodes` directly, matching line 1232.

No duplicate PRs found.

Testing:
- `uvx pre-commit run ruff-format --files vllm/v1/attention/backends/rocm_aiter_fa.py` (passes locally)
- Previous CI failure only requested the matching `ruff format` whitespace change on this line; this follow-up commit applies that exact diff.

AI assistance was used for code search and CI log review; all changes reviewed by the human submitter.

Signed-off-by: Bortlesboat <bortstheboat@gmail.com>

**Reference:** The correct `cu_seqlens_q` slicing is confirmed by the upstream AITER `unified_attention` implementation:
https://github.com/ROCm/aiter/blob/bc5ea32c19c604cda2f4781a97cb04d5fc494543/aiter/ops/triton/_triton_kernels/attention/unified_attention.py#L426

prs.title: [KV Offload] Pass request context
prs.author: omerpaz95
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Pass per-request context (`kv_transfer_params`) through the CPU offloading manager interface.

The `OffloadingManager` ABC and `CPUOffloadingManager` now accept a `ReqContext` argument on `lookup`, `prepare_load`, and `prepare_store`. `ReqContext` wraps `request.kv_transfer_params` (a free-form dict set via `sampling_params.extra_args`) and is constructed at each call site in `OffloadingConnectorScheduler`.

This is wiring-only: `ReqContext` is accepted but not yet consumed by `CPUOffloadingManager`.

Structural changes:
- `ReqContext` dataclass is defined in `vllm/v1/kv_offload/abstract.py` (shared layer) rather than `cpu/manager.py`, so other `OffloadingManager` implementations can use it without importing from the CPU-specific module.

## Test Plan

```bash
# Lint
cd vllm
pre-commit run

# Scheduler unit tests
pytest tests/v1/kv_connector/test_offloading_scheduler.py -v -s
```

## Test Result

Pre-commit (ruff, mypy, all hooks): all passed.

---
